metadata.title: States PCS: Nagaland GK Questions for Nagaland PSC Examinations

questions.question: Which of the following is the highest mountain peak in Nagaland?
questions.explanation: Mount Saramati, at 3,826 meters, is the highest peak in Nagaland, situated on the Indo-Myanmar border in the Tuensang district.

questions.question: Which is the longest and most important river in Nagaland?
questions.explanation: The Doyang River is the longest in Nagaland, serving as a key tributary of the Brahmaputra and supporting irrigation, hydropower, and fisheries.

questions.question: Which district in Nagaland is renowned for its terraced rice fields and hilly landscapes?
questions.explanation: Zunheboto is prominent for its terraced rice fields on steep hills, where the Sumi Nagas employ traditional cultivation methods suited to the hilly terrain.

questions.question: Which type of soil is predominantly found in the hilly regions of Nagaland?
questions.explanation: Loamy soil, rich in organic matter and derived from weathered rocks, is most common in Nagaland’s hilly areas, suitable for jhum and terrace farming.

questions.question: Which river forms a part of the boundary between Nagaland and Assam?
questions.explanation: The Dhansiri River marks part of the western boundary between Nagaland and Assam; it is a Brahmaputra tributary.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the predominant climate of Nagaland?
questions.explanation: Nagaland experiences a humid monsoon climate with heavy rainfall in summer and cool winters, supporting lush forests and rich biodiversity.

questions.question: In which district of Nagaland is the largest natural lake, Shilloi Lake, located?
questions.explanation: Shilloi Lake, the largest natural lake in Nagaland, is situated in Phek district.

questions.question: Which mountain pass links Nagaland in India to the Sagaing Region of Myanmar?
questions.explanation: Lungwa Pass, near Mon district, connects Nagaland to the Sagaing Region; Longwa village straddles both countries.

questions.question: Which type of natural vegetation predominantly covers the higher altitudes of Nagaland?
questions.explanation: Pine forests, especially Khasi pine, dominate the higher altitudes of Nagaland and are vital for timber and local livelihoods.

questions.question: Which town in Nagaland is situated at the confluence of the Chathe and Doyang rivers?
questions.explanation: Dimapur is located at the junction of the Chathe and Doyang rivers, making it a strategic commercial and ecological hub in Nagaland.
